response.justification: While the candidate possesses extensive overall software development experience and some relevant exposure to investment practices, they critically lack the mandatory 'strong hands-on C++ development experience (5–10 years) on Windows platform' required for this role, showing only 6 months of explicit C++ work. Their primary professional background is in web technologies and Dlang, which does not align with the core C++ requirement for critical capital markets systems.
strengths: Over 19 years of diverse software development experience.
Demonstrated experience in investment practices/capital markets (RIDE Capital).
Proficiency in modern development practices (CI/CD, TDD) and various technologies (Node.js, TypeScript, Java).
concerns: Only 6 months of explicit professional C++ development on Windows, falling significantly short of the 5-10 years mandatory requirement.
Lack of specific experience with SQL Server or Oracle, despite general SQL knowledge being listed.
Primary career focus has been on web development and Dlang, not C++ for critical financial systems.
